events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: grounding electrode system at rough-complete stage, observed field condition is "missing supplemental electrode." NEC 250.50 requires all grounding electrodes present at each building/structure be bonded together to form the grounding electrode system. NEC 250.52 lists types of electrodes. NEC 250.53 covers installation requirements, including supplemental electrode requirements in some cases (e.g., 250.53(A)(2) for rod/pipe/plate electrodes - if resistance is high, supplemental electrode needed; also 250.53(D) for grounding electrode conductor, etc.). Actually 250.53(A)(2) "Supplemental Electrode Required" - a single rod/pipe/plate electrode does not have resistance ≤25 ohms, a supplemental electrode must be added.

Missing supplemental electrode = non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: medium-high (grounding system integrity compromised, safety hazard). Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective (missing component). Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 85%. Confidence high.
